channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former Presidential candidate in Taiwan, Ko Wen-je, indicted for corruption
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Prosecutors in Taiwan indicted former presidential candidate and Taiwan People's Party founder Ko Wen-je on corruption charges Thursday, accusing him of taking bribes during his time as mayor of the island's capital.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A survivor of the worst gang attack on Haitian journalists says colleagues were cut down by bullets
item.description: A survivor of the worst gang attack on Haitian journalists in recent memory described Wednesday seeing colleagues cut down by bullets and reporters with head and chest wounds going an hour or more without help.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE LUXAMA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Return your 2024 holiday gifts by these deadlines
item.description: To lessen the pressure a little bit, major brands, including Best Buy, Macy's, and Walmart, have extended their return policies.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Body found in wheel well of United Airlines Christmas Eve flight from Chicago to Maui
item.description: An investigation is underway after a body was discovered in the wheel well of an airplane that landed in Maui after taking off from Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blake Lively's claims that movie co-star launched smear campaign gets support in publicist's suit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Blake Lively’s claims that “It Ends With Us” director and co-star Justin Baldoni unleashed a smear campaign against her have gained support from a new lawsuit that Baldoni’s former publicist filed against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trial of man accused in Trump assassination attempt in Florida pushed back to September
item.description: A man accused of attempting to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump in South Florida won't be tried until September 2025, a federal judge ruled this week.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Airliner carrying 67 people crashes in Kazakhstan
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jackpot for Mega Millions over $1 billion after no numbers match on Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: Hang onto those stocking-stuffer lottery tickets. The Mega Millions jackpot has surpassed $1 billion after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. That means the estimated jackpot for Friday's drawing is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Centuries-old angels uncovered beneath layers of paint at Boston church made famous by Paul Revere
item.description: Mural conservators are working to uncover angels hidden under layers of paint for more than a century at the Boston church made famous by Paul Revere.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former defense official makes earth-shattering UFO revelation as unexplained drones leave millions on edge
item.description: Recent testimony and several reports have exposed possible UFO sightings across the country amid national attention on apparent drone observations in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US census data shows how Middle Eastern and Hispanic residents differ in racial identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau currently is getting public feedback about how it should tally responses from people into new race and ethnicity groups.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico to open 25 shelters for deported migrants
item.description: Baja California Gov. Marina del Pilar Ávila Olmeda announced the Mexican government will open 25 new shelters to house deported migrants.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Richard Perry, record producer behind 'You're So Vain' and other hits, dies at 82
item.description: Richard Perry, a hitmaking record producer with a flair for both standards and contemporary sounds whose many successes included Carly Simon’s “You’re So Vain,” Rod Stewart’s “The Great American Songbook” series and a Ringo Starr album featuring all four Beatles, died Tuesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illegal immigrant involved in suspected DUI crash that claimed life of Georgia realtor
item.description: A Georgia realtor is dead following a crash that allegedly involved an illegal immigrant from Honduras, who officials say was also an underage drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mega Millions jackpot climbs to $1B ahead of Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot could become the Christmas gift of a lifetime for a lucky lottery player ahead of Tuesday night’s drawing. The jackpot now stands at an estimated value of $1 billion, California Lottery officials announced on X.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Heavy travel day off to a rough start after American Airlines briefly grounds all flights
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — American Airlines briefly grounded flights nationwide Tuesday because of a technical problem just as the Christmas travel season kicked into overdrive and winter weather threatened more potential problems for those planning to fly or drive.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: Check your medicine cabinets: A brand of eye drops sold nationally has been recalled due to the presence of fungus, which may cause vision damage.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: California residents on edge as high surf and flooding threats persist on Christmas Eve
item.description: Persistent high surf and flooding threats along California's coast have residents on high alert a day after a major storm was blamed for one man's death and the partial collapse of a pier.
item.dc:creator: Martha Mendoza And Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man arraigned on murder charges in NYC subway death fanned flames with a shirt, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors say a man accused of burning a woman to death inside a New York City subway train used a shirt to fan the flames, causing her to become engulfed.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: FAYETTEVILLE, Ark. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing “harmful” materials to minors. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Where is Santa? How to track his Christmas 2024 journey
item.description: Santa Claus, St. Nicholas, Babbo Natale, Père Noël — whatever you call him, the man clad in red will be here soon.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Van bursts into flames inside California tunnel
item.description: Emergency crews responded to a vehicle fire inside the Caldecott Tunnel in the San Francisco Bay Area on Monday morning, according to the California Highway Patrol. Eastbound Highway 24 is shut down inside Bore 1 after a minivan caught fire at around 9:54 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, WFLA, NBC
